title: Sunday, March 4
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver | March 4, 2018 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This Sunday falls in the Third Sunday of Lent (Lenten Season), occurring 21 days before Easter Sunday (April 1, 2018). The Revised Common Lectionary readings would have included Exodus 20:1-17 (the Ten Commandments), Psalm 19, 1 Corinthians 1:18-25, and John 2:13-22 (Jesus cleansing the temple). The sermon date places the congregation deep in the penitential season of Lent, traditionally a time of reflection, fasting, and spiritual preparation for Holy Week.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

Bishop Robert J. O'Neill was serving as the Bishop of Colorado during this period, leading the Diocese of Colorado. The broader Episcopal Church was in the period following General Convention 2015, focused on implementing resolutions and managing ongoing theological tensions around marriage and sexuality. No major diocesan controversies or bishop elections were occurring in early March 2018, though the Church continued navigating post-Obergefell marriage equality implementation.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical early March weather patterns with the possibility of spring snow (Denver averaged 4-6 inches of snow in March historically). The Colorado Legislature was in active session (January-May 2018 session), debating bills on education funding, healthcare, and other state priorities. Saint John's Cathedral sits in downtown Denver's Capitol Hill neighborhood, which was experiencing ongoing gentrification pressures and demographic changes during the 2010s.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The nation was consumed by the aftermath of the Parkland school shooting (February 14), with nationwide student walkouts and gun control protests occurring throughout February and into early March. The Trump administration was engaged in escalating trade tensions, with tariff announcements on steel and aluminum imminent (officially announced March 8). The #MeToo movement remained a dominant cultural conversation, with ongoing revelations and workplace accountability measures across industries.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia was preparing for its presidential election (March 18), with Vladimir Putin's reelection widely expected; this dominated international coverage. The Winter Olympics had just concluded in PyeongChang, South Korea (February 25), with lingering geopolitical tensions between North and South Korea. Tensions in Syria continued with ongoing civil war and reports of chemical weapons use by the Assad regime, which would escalate dramatically weeks later (April 2018 chemical attack).

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was experiencing acute cultural polarization and anxiety about gun violence, particularly affecting young people and schools. The broader mood was characterized by political division, social media-driven activism, and generational conflict over gun rights and school safety. In sports, March Madness college basketball was about to begin (Selection Sunday March 11), and the Denver Nuggets were in their NBA season; the Broncos' offseason was underway after a disappointing 2017 season.
